sections.paragraph:
θερμαίνω (θερμός; Hom. et al.; LXX; TestSol 18:18 P; Ar. 6, 1; POxy 1778, 30) 1 aor. impv. θέρμανον Sir 38:17; subj. 2 sg. θερμάνῃς TestSol 18:18 P; in our lit. only θερμαίνομαι (PSI 406, 37 [III b.c.]; Jos., Bell. 3, 274) impf. ἐθερμαινόμην; 1 aor. ἐθερμάνθην LXX warm oneself at a fire (Is 44:16) Mk 14:67; J 18:18ab, 25. πρὸς τὸ φῶς at the fire Mk 14:54. Of clothing (Hg 1:6; Job 31:20) θερμαίνεσθε dress warmly! keep warm! Js 2:16.—DELG s.v. θέρομαι 3.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

aor. aor. = aorist

impv. impv. = imperative

subj. subj. = subjunctive; subject

  1. sg. = singular

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

impf. impf. = imperfect

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 454
